hypothesis: Incorporating explicit solvent molecule interactions through a hybrid implicit-explicit solvation scheme during ChEMBL3D dataset construction—where first-shell water molecules are treated explicitly while bulk solvent remains implicit—would enable LoQI to generate conformers that more accurately reproduce protein-bound ligand geometries in the Platinum Diverse benchmark, reducing median RMSD below 0.35 Å at ensemble size N=250, because the current CPCM-only training distribution systematically underrepresents the directional hydrogen-bonding geometries that stabilize bioactive conformations in aqueous protein environments.
